rationale: The det.rs file was missing imports/`verus!{}` wrapping/main; I added these outside the marker region and supplied `optional_value_view`. The proof obligation requires deriving `r1->Some_0 == r2->Some_0` (Verus spec equality on `Vec<u8>`) from the hypothesis that `optional_value_view(ov) == optional_value_view(r1)` and `... == optional_value_view(r2)`, which only yields sequence-level (view) equality `r1->Some_0@ == r2->Some_0@`. Verus does not provide an axiom `v1@ == v2@ ==> v1 == v2` for `Vec` (Vec is an `external_type_specification` opaque wrapper around `alloc::vec::Vec`; its abstract content beyond the view is unspecified). I verified this empirically with a minimal Verus reproduction: `proof fn test(v1: Vec<u8>, v2: Vec<u8>) requires v1@ == v2@ ensures v1 == v2 {}` fails the postcondition even with `broadcast use vstd::std_specs::vec::group_vec_axioms`, with `=~=`, and with `deep_view` reasoning.
